Corporate Web Architecture Sprint

Master Services Agreement

Velocity by Calyvent
Master Services Agreement

1. Production Timeline Engine & Kickoff Contingencies

The design and development cycle for custom software and web properties engineered by Velocity is a minimum of seven (7) business days up to a maximum of nine (9) business days from the formal kickoff date. Under no circumstances does the project timeline begin upon form submission. The formal kickoff date is uniquely instantiated only when:

  1. One hundred percent (100%) of the financial considerations outlined in the Custom Quote have successfully cleared the transaction gateway.
  2. The Client has populated the required asset storage bucket with all corporate vector logos, high-fidelity images, and validated copy assets required for platform assembly.

Velocity retains the absolute right to deliver the finished digital platform ahead of schedule. If the Client takes longer than twenty-four (24) hours to respond to a critical operational inquiry or clarity ping, the production timeline is automatically paused, and Velocity reserves the right to re-schedule the target delivery window.

2. Payment Architecture & Non-Negotiable Refund Schedule

Velocity by Calyvent operates on a strict allocation model, prioritizing a dedicated elite engineering sprint for each approved contract. Consequently, the transaction mechanics enforce a one hundred percent (100%) upfront, non-mitigatable financial deployment. All capital payments transacted are non-refundable and final. The Client possesses only two (2) narrow paths of financial restitution:

  1. Immediate 24-Hour Revocation: The Client may request a complete contract termination via documented written notification to the Velocity ecosystem within exactly twenty-four (24) hours of initial payment completion.
  2. Failure to Supply: If Velocity fails to engineer and provide an interactive, functional build iteration of the digital web property within nine (9) calendar days of the formal kickoff date. Any delay, asset omission, structural alteration, or slow feedback loops by the Client immediately voids this Failure to Supply clause in its entirety.

3. Consolidated Revision Allocations & Complex Feedback Protocol

Velocity provides a maximum allowance of three (3) iterative rounds of platform revision. To preserve operational velocity and avoid margin degradation from endless cosmetic micro-tweaks, the iteration phase is subject to strict formatting constraints:

  1. Anti-Token Restriction: The submission of isolated, sequential micro-requests (e.g., requesting the repositioning of a singular interface button, then waiting for execution, then requesting a font size alteration) is completely prohibited. Individual piece-meal feedback voids the active iteration round.
  2. Comprehensive Consolidation Directive: The Client is instructed and legally bound to comprehensively audit the interactive build iteration across all screen configurations and viewpoints. The Client must compile all requested adjustments into a singular, thorough, all-inclusive diagnostic document.
  3. Medium of Delivery: Feedback data must be delivered as a single, consolidated text brief, or via an explicitly clear voice note or voice-to-text transcript detailing every necessary structural alteration across all pages. Any structural modifications requested beyond the three (3) allocated rounds will be auto-billed to the Client at Velocity’s standard emergency developer tier rate of two hundred and fifty dollars ($250.00) per hour.

4. Intellectual Property Distribution & Structural Liability Proxy

Upon the successful electronic verification and clearing of all open financial liabilities and the formal close of the design sprint, all copyrights, intellectual property assertions, and absolute domain ownership properties of the compiled production web application transfer unconditionally to the Client.

  1. Continuous Optimization Exception: If the Client enters into an active ongoing Retainer, Continuous Maintenance, or Digital Optimization Agreement with Velocity for ongoing structural updates, Velocity explicitly retains partial structural architecture and design framework engineering rights.
  2. Liability Shield: This partial right configuration serves as an ironclad operational shield. Velocity by Calyvent is held completely harmless, and the Client waives all paths of structural litigation, malpractice assertion, or business disruption indemnity claims if subsequent third-party platform modifications, server-side code alterations, dependency failures, or user-driven code breaking damages the operational integrity of the digital platform.
